ddls:
CREATE TABLE "job_postings" (
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job posting */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the category of the job */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Title of the job */,
  "job_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the job */,
  "page_link" TEXT /* Link to the job application page */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location of the job */,
  "run_date" TEXT /* Date the job was last run or updated */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the job posting was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date the job posting was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the job posting (e.g., open, closed, filled) */,
  "application_count" INTEGER /* Number of applications received for the job posting */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the job posting */,
  "job_type" TEXT /* The type of job (full-time, part-time, internship, etc.) */,
  "experience_level" TEXT /* The experience level required for the job (entry-level, mid-level, senior-level, etc.) */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* The salary range for the job */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* The education level required for the job (high school, bachelor's degree, master's degree, etc.) */,
  "skills_required" TEXT /* The skills required for the job */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_postings_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the location */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., recruiter, hiring manager, admin) */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job posting the application is for */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who submitted the application */,
  "application_date" TEXT /* Date the application was submitted */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the application (e.g., new, reviewed, rejected, hired) */,
  "resume_path" TEXT /* File path to the applicant's resume */,
  "cover_letter_path" TEXT /* File path to the applicant's cover letter */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date the application was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("application_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_status FOREIGN KEY ("status") REFERENCES application_status ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES job_postings ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "application_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_requirements" (
  "requirement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each requirement */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job posting the requirement is for */,
  "requirement_name" TEXT /* Name of the requirement */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the requirement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("requirement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_requirements_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES job_postings ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_benefits" (
  "benefit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each benefit */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job posting the benefit is for */,
  "benefit_name" TEXT /* Name of the benefit */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the benefit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("benefit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_benefits_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES job_postings ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_posting_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job posting the version is for */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the job posting */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the changes in this version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_posting_versions_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES job_postings ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "job_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job posting being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the job posting */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the job posting was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, apply) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_access_logs_job_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_id") REFERENCES job_postings ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_skills" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who possesses the skill */,
  "skill" TEXT /* Name of the skill */,
  "level" TEXT /* Level of proficiency in the skill (e.g., beginner, intermediate, advanced) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "skill")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_education" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who has the education history */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Level of education (e.g., high school, bachelor's degree, master's degree) */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Name of the institution where the education was obtained */,
  "graduation_date" TEXT /* Date of graduation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "education_level")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_experience" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who has the experience history */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Title of the job */,
  "company" TEXT /* Name of the company where the experience was gained */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the job */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the job */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "job_title")
)
